evals.name: crypto-ohlcv-composite-grain
evals.prompt: Build a Cube model for the ohlcv_1h mart in cube/model/cubes/. I want to be able to chart price and volume over time in the Cube Playground.
evals.expected_output: cube/model/cubes/ohlcv_1h.yml with sql_table: marts.ohlcv_1h. Dimensions include symbol and candle_open_time, both marked primary_key: true (composite grain per dbt meta.grain). Measures include count plus avg/max on open/high/low/close and sum (or avg) on volume/notional_volume/trade_count, each with a description matching the phrasing pattern. No ingested_at dimension (the mart doesn't have one, so none should be invented).
expectations: cube/model/cubes/ohlcv_1h.yml exists and is valid YAML
sql_table is exactly 'marts.ohlcv_1h'
Both symbol and candle_open_time dimensions are marked primary_key: true
candle_open_time dimension has type: time
A count measure exists with type: count
At least one measure exists for each of open, high, low, close (avg and/or max)
volume, notional_volume and trade_count each have at least one measure
No ingested_at dimension or measure appears anywhere in the file
Every dimension/measure name referenced via sql: corresponds to an actual column in the ohlcv_1h mart
The user was asked which mart to build on and/or which metrics to include, rather than the file being written silently with no interaction

evals.name: statsbomb-match-summary-sum-measures
evals.prompt: Add a Cube model on top of the sb_match_summary dbt mart so we can build a matches dashboard in Cube.
evals.expected_output: cube/model/cubes/sb_match_summary.yml with sql_table: marts.sb_match_summary. match_id is the sole primary_key dimension. Dimensions include competition_name, season_name, home_team_name, away_team_name, match_result, competition_stage; ingested_at is excluded. Measures include count plus sum-style measures (not avg) on the total_* columns (total_shots, total_passes, total_dribbles, total_pressures, total_carries) since these are per-match totals being aggregated across matches, plus something on home_score/away_score/goal_difference (avg is defensible here). Descriptions on both dimensions and measures are adapted from (not copied verbatim in the case of the top-level description) the dbt model's description/columns, with the 'Ordered by match_date descending' clause dropped from the top-level description.
expectations: cube/model/cubes/sb_match_summary.yml exists and is valid YAML
sql_table is exactly 'marts.sb_match_summary'
match_id is the only dimension marked primary_key: true
ingested_at does not appear as a dimension
A count measure exists
total_shots, total_passes, total_dribbles, total_pressures and total_carries each have a measure of type sum rather than avg
The top-level cube description does not contain the phrase 'Ordered by' or 'descending'
Dimension descriptions are non-empty and topically match the corresponding dbt column description
Every dimension/measure sql: value corresponds to an actual column in the sb_match_summary mart

evals.name: statsbomb-player-stats-composite-and-nullable
evals.prompt: I need a Cube model for sb_player_stats so I can slice player activity stats by country and team.
evals.expected_output: cube/model/cubes/sb_player_stats.yml with sql_table: marts.sb_player_stats. player_id and team both marked primary_key: true (composite grain). country_name included as a dimension despite being nullable. Measures include count plus sum-style measures on total_events/total_passes/total_shots/total_dribbles/total_pressures/total_carries/total_duration_minutes (dataset totals, not per-game averages) and likely sum or avg on lineup_appearances/matches_in_events. Descriptions follow the fixed phrasing pattern from the skill's conventions reference.
expectations: cube/model/cubes/sb_player_stats.yml exists and is valid YAML
sql_table is exactly 'marts.sb_player_stats'
Both player_id and team dimensions are marked primary_key: true
country_name appears as a dimension despite being nullable in the source data
A count measure exists
total_events, total_passes, total_shots, total_dribbles, total_pressures, total_carries and total_duration_minutes each have a sum-type measure (not avg)
Every dimension/measure sql: value corresponds to an actual column in the sb_player_stats mart
The skill introspected the live table/dbt docs rather than inventing column names from memory (no hallucinated columns)
